response.body: {"success":true,"message":"Tool submitted successfully! We'll review it soon."}

submitted_payload.name: Ralph Workflow
submitted_payload.notes: Free and open-source composable loop framework and AI orchestrator for structured autonomous coding. Codeberg is the primary repo; GitHub is the mirror.

why_this_action: Shared artifacts still show distribution_and_message_to_primary_repo_conversion as the live bottleneck, Apollo is already in a measurement window until 2026-05-30, Reddit remains structurally blocked/high-risk, and ToolShelf.dev exposed a directly relevant developer-tool submission API that can send qualified evaluators straight to the primary Codeberg repo.
shared_findings_reused: MARKETING_WORKFLOW_PRINCIPLES.md: prioritize Codeberg-first distribution and proof-led positioning
FOUR_MARKETING_QUESTIONS.md: preserved what it is, who it is for, why different, why now
ADOPTION_FUNNEL_NEXT.md: bias toward free-use path and proof-led conversion instead of more generic posting
market_intelligence_latest.json: reused positioning truths and category fit versus other coding-agent tools
channel_discovery.json: ToolShelf was already identified as a promising easy-submit lane but had not yet been converted into a live Codeberg-primary submission

summary: updated the validated ToolShelf autonomous-submit note to reflect the real successful 2026-05-23 execution
refreshed channel_discovery.json so future runs see ToolShelf as actually submitted, not merely inferred from a probe

measurement_window.success_signal: live ToolShelf.dev listing or backlink pointing evaluators to the Codeberg primary repo
measurement_window.kill_condition: If no listing or backlink evidence appears by 2026-06-06, replace this lane with a different untouched curator/directory surface instead of resubmitting ToolShelf.
